name: Sauteed Eggplant W/Tomato and Onion Topping (5)
recipeInstructions:
1. Cut eggplant into 1/2 inch slices and sprinkle with a little salt on both sides to draw out water. Let the slices drain in a colander for 30 minutes.
2. While the eggplant is draining, cook the onion in 1 tablespoon olive oil over moderate heat, stirring until golden. Stir in garlic, tomatoes, oregano, and wine; simmer, stirring occasionally, for 10 minutes or until liquid is almost evaporated.
3. Stir in Parmesan cheese, parsley, salt, and pepper to taste. Transfer the topping to a bowl, cover, and keep warm.
4. Brush off any remaining salt from the eggplant surfaces, rinse quickly, and pat dry.
5. Heat the remaining olive oil in a skillet until hot but not smoking. Cook the eggplant, browning on one side, then turn and reduce heat. Cook an additional 5 minutes or more until tender. Transfer to a plate, spoon the tomato and onion topping over it, and serve with additional cheese.
